problem.instruction: Read the passage in <A> and the teacher's reflection log in <B>, and follow the directions.
problem.passage:
<A>
Mr. Jeong, an English teacher, was tasked with evaluating speaking assessment items in his students' final exam. Reviewing key principles of speaking assessment, he noted the following:
✔ Clarity: Prompts should be straightforward to avoid confusion.
✔ Authenticity: Speaking tasks should mirror real-life communication, enabling students to demonstrate natural language use.
✔ Integrated Skills Assessment: Tasks should assess speaking alongside other skills, such as listening comprehension, to reflect communicative performance.
✔ Practicality: Test items should be feasible and manageable in terms of the time spent in assessment.

<B>
Teacher's Reflection Log
After reviewing the items, I felt that the two items had some good and bad points. Item 1 asked students to describe a memorable experience that they had with a friend, including details such as when it happened, what they did, and why it was memorable. After observing students' responses, I realized that this item resembled a conversation topic in real-life contexts. However, I regret that I didn't set time limits for the item and it took too much time to score it, which made the assessment difficult to manage.

For Item 2, after looking at a picture of a busy street, students were asked to describe what they saw. Most of the students did very well on this task because the item clearly described what sort of response was desired. I think this item was effective in assessing pronunciation, one of the criteria for assessing speaking skills. However, next time I want to add some more items such as asking students to listen to a short audio and discuss their opinions. It might be more challenging but I believe I can assess multiple skills in the test.

Based on <A>, identify the speaking assessment principles applied in Item 1 and Item 2 in <B>, respectively. Then, explain how each principle was applied in each item with evidence from <B>.

steps.label: 범위 설정 — 말하기 평가 4원칙 확인
steps.explanation:
<A>에 제시된 4가지 말하기 평가 원칙:
- Clarity: 프롬프트가 명확해야 혼란 방지
- Authenticity: 실생활 의사소통을 반영해 자연스러운 언어 사용 가능
- Integrated Skills Assessment: 다른 기술과 통합 평가
- Practicality: 시간·관리 측면에서 실현 가능해야 함
<B>에서 Item 1과 Item 2 각각에 적용된 원칙 하나씩 식별 요구.

steps.label: 이론 ↔ 지문 대응 — Item 1
steps.explanation:
Item 1: 학생이 친구와 기억에 남는 경험을 묘사. 교사 로그: 'this item resembled a conversation topic in real-life contexts.'
→ Authenticity 정의: 'Speaking tasks should mirror real-life communication, enabling students to demonstrate natural language use.'
→ 정확히 대응. Item 1 = Authenticity ✓

단, 교사는 시간 제한 미설정으로 채점 어려웠다고 언급 → Practicality 부족이지만 이는 질문이 '적용된 원칙' 요구이므로 Authenticity 서술.

steps.label: 이론 ↔ 지문 대응 — Item 2
steps.explanation:
Item 2: 혼잡한 거리 그림을 보고 묘사. 교사 로그: 'the item clearly described what sort of response was desired.'
→ Clarity 정의: 'Prompts should be straightforward to avoid confusion.'
→ 정확히 대응. Item 2 = Clarity ✓

교사는 추후 듣기 클립 추가 희망 → Integrated Skills Assessment를 목표로 하지만 현재 Item 2에 적용된 원칙은 Clarity.

steps.label: 확정 근거 — 지문 직접 인용
steps.explanation:
Authenticity 근거: 'this item resembled a conversation topic in real-life contexts' — 실생활 대화 주제와의 유사성 명시.
Clarity 근거: 'the item clearly described what sort of response was desired' + 'most of the students did very well' — 명확한 프롬프트가 수행을 촉진.

steps.label: 전략 메모
steps.explanation:
말하기 평가 원칙 식별 패턴:
① 교사 로그에서 '실생활/자연스러운' 키워드 → Authenticity
② '명확히 설명됐다/혼란 없었다' 키워드 → Clarity
③ '시간 초과/관리 어려움' → Practicality 결함 (식별 문제에선 적용된 원칙, 결함 원칙 구분 주의)

함정: Practicality 결함(Item 1 시간 제한 미설정)이 명시되지만, '적용된 원칙'은 Authenticity — 결함과 적용 원칙 혼동 금지.

key_terms.definition_en: A speaking assessment principle requiring that tasks mirror real-life communication to enable students to demonstrate natural language use.
key_terms.definition_ko: 말하기 평가 원칙 중 하나로, 과제가 실생활 의사소통을 반영하여 자연스러운 언어 사용을 보여줄 수 있어야 함.
key_terms.exam_usage: Item 1: 실생활 대화 주제(memorable experience with friends)로 출제

key_terms.definition_en: A speaking assessment principle requiring that prompts be straightforward to avoid student confusion.
key_terms.definition_ko: 말하기 평가 원칙 중 하나로, 학생들의 혼란을 피하기 위해 프롬프트가 명확해야 함.
key_terms.exam_usage: Item 2: 그림 프롬프트가 기대 응답을 명확히 제시함

model_answer.essay_content: Authenticity is applied to Item 1 in that the task topic about a memorable experience with friends resembles a real-life conversation context. Clarity is applied to Item 2 since the picture prompt of a busy street clearly described what sort of response was desired, enabling students to perform well.

scoring_criteria.criterion: Item 1 근거: 'resembled a conversation topic in real-life contexts' 언급
Item 2 근거: 'clearly described what sort of response was desired' 언급

variants.label: 답안 A
variants.content: Authenticity is applied to Item 1 because the conversation topic about a memorable experience with friends reflects real-life contexts. Clarity is applied to Item 2 because the use of a prompt, 'a picture of a busy street', enables students to clearly describe what they observed.

variants.label: 답안 B
variants.content: Item 1 applies Authenticity: the teacher notes that this item 'resembled a conversation topic in real-life contexts,' highlighting its alignment with authentic communication. Item 2 applies Clarity: the teacher observes that 'the item clearly described what sort of response was desired,' indicating that clarity was successfully applied.

references.author: Brown, H. D.
references.title: Language Assessment: Principles and Classroom Practices
references.excerpt: Principles of speaking assessment include authenticity — tasks should mirror real-life communication contexts so that learners can demonstrate natural language use — and clarity — prompts should be straightforward and unambiguous to prevent learner confusion. Practicality requires that tests be feasible in terms of administration time and scoring demands.

references.author: Brown, H. D. & Abeywickrama, P.
references.title: Language Assessment: Principles and Classroom Practices (2nd ed.)
references.excerpt: Authentic assessment tasks reflect the conditions and constraints of real-world language use. Authenticity in speaking assessment means that the topic, context, or interaction pattern closely resembles those found in communicative situations outside the classroom. A lack of practicality — for instance, failing to set time limits — may render an otherwise valid item unmanageable in practice.

source.concept_explanation:
Brown (2004) speaking assessment principles:

- Clarity: Prompts should be straightforward to avoid confusion — 명확하고 단순한 지시문으로 학생 혼란 방지. Item 2의 사진 프롬프트가 기대 응답을 명확히 제시.
- Authenticity: Tasks should mirror real-life communication — 실생활 의사소통과 유사한 과제. Item 1의 '친구와의 기억 묘사' 주제가 해당.
- Integrated Skills Assessment: Assess speaking alongside other skills — 듣기·읽기 등 다른 기술과 통합. 교사가 향후 추가 희망한 '듣기 클립 활용' 방식.
- Practicality: Feasible and manageable in terms of time — 시간·채점 면에서 관리 가능해야 함. Item 1에서 시간 제한 미설정 → Practicality 결함 사례.

식별 핵심: 교사 로그 내 '실생활 유사' 표현 → Authenticity; '명확한 프롬프트/기대 응답 명시' 표현 → Clarity.
